data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: The design limits choices to reduce user decision time, facilitating faster navigation.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: UI elements are designed larger and closer to expected user focus areas for improved interaction speed.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Content is broken down into manageable pieces, aiding memory retention and processing.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Users are offered familiar navigation patterns to enhance intuitive interaction.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Visual appeal is leveraged to enhance perceived usability and user satisfaction.
